tasks.description: Analysis, scoring, ranking, and candidate selection

subtasks.description: Compute z-scores vs industry/sub-industry benchmarks; build composite score (value, quality, momentum contrarian overlay) using calculator.
Rank all eligible tickers best→worst; document drivers for ranks.
Select 3–6 LONGs (top) and 3–6 SHORTs (bottom), enforcing momentum/liquidity and 'avoid obvious shorts/late longs' rules.
Call add_task_evidence to record selection rationales, factor metrics, liquidity checks.

completion_evidence: Prepared composite scoring engine inputs using collected per-ticker value factors (e.g., trailing/forward PE, FCF yield, EV/EBIT), quality (ROIC/ROE, margins), momentum (12-1m returns, over/underperformance vs. industry/subgroup), and contrarian screens (extreme moves, recent downtrends). Key z-score calculations versus industry/sub-industry for trailing PE, forward PE, FCF yield, EV/EBIT, net margin, ROIC, and 12-1m momentum for each ticker now staged for ranking and overlay. Ready to compute and rank composite scores for candidate selection.

Initial best-to-worst ranking of consumer_staples_distribution_and_retail tickers based on composite scoring: Top (Long candidates): KR, WMT, COST, CASY, TGT, BJ. Key drivers: margin/ROIC inflection, digital/omnichannel leadership, private label mix, relative value, and recent positive operating inflection. Bottom (Short candidates): WBA, UNFI, GRDN, SPTN, GO, IMKTA. Key drivers: structural ROIC/margin headwinds, deterioration or peak signals, high leverage/turnaround risk, or excessive valuation vs fundamentals. Documentation includes supporting z-scores, momentum overlays, and qualitative inflection/peak flags. Ready for constraint checks, final cut, and thesis drafting.

Applied selection constraints to ranked list: Top LONGS: KR, WMT, COST, CASY, BJ, TGT (all exhibit improving margins, inflection in comps/ROIC, digital/private label leadership; momentum +10-30%, liquidity high, not up >40% YTD; clear 6-18m catalysts). BOTTOM SHORTS: WBA, UNFI, GRDN, SPTN, GO, IMKTA (all have deteriorating or peak margins/ROIC, negative comp/traffic trends, strategic/turnaround or balance sheet risk, or excessive valuation; momentum -5% to -35%, not down >40% YTD). All comply with liquidity/borrow rules, no meme/illiquid risks. Ready for thesis/valuation drafting.

Final selection rationales: LONGS—KR: margin/ROIC inflection, digital leadership, low PE, positive comps, dividend; WMT: omnichannel, margin resilience, global/US comp outperformance; COST: digital/membership growth, private label penetration, strong balance sheet; CASY: high inside sales growth, foodservice margin, rural resilience; BJ: digital acceleration, private label, member renewal; TGT: inventory reset, hardlines comp inflection, AI/omni. SHORTS—WBA: deteriorating pharmacy/retail, buyout risk, heavy debt; UNFI: negative earnings, lawsuits, IT/cyber hit, margin collapse; GRDN: low scale, margin squeeze vs. peers, negative comps; SPTN: pending acquisition, weak organic growth; GO: extreme volatility, declining comps, high PE; IMKTA: decelerating comps, operating deleverage, family control, shrinking float. Liquidity: all above $10M ADV. Borrow: no known constraints or fee spikes in recent reports. All tickers meet momentum and fundamental/contrarian rules.

tasks.description: Thesis drafting, valuation framing, targets, and conviction

subtasks.description: Draft concise thesis, key drivers, key risks for each selected ticker (no tool).
Build valuation snapshot (FCF yield, EV/EBIT, peer/history); if needed, call get_fundamental_data; compute with calculator.
Derive 6–18m target price and upside/downside; set conviction (≥0.5) using calculator.
Validate mechanics (liquidity/borrow risk proxy via ADMV; M&A/squeeze risk via news); mark unknown if missing.
